settings.headline: Dry Clothes Faster Indoors — Without Relying on the Weather
settings.subheadline:
Premium ceiling-mounted laundry racks with smart controls, heated drying, lighting, and space-saving convenience for modern Australian homes.

Dry Wrinkle-Free with Nuracom.

settings.title: Dry indoors, rain or shine
settings.text: Never plan washing around the weather again — dry anywhere, any time.

settings.title: Faster than line drying
settings.text: Heated airflow and elevated positioning dry clothes up to 3× faster than a line.

settings.title: Under $1 per drying cycle
settings.text: A full 2-hour cycle costs 57¢ on Breeze and Aero, 64¢ on Stratos — roughly a third of what a tumble dryer charges you per load.

settings.title: Save floor space
settings.text: Lifts out of the way when not in use — your laundry stays clean and functional.

settings.title: Premium lighting and smart controls
settings.text: Built-in lighting, remote control, and automation that actually feels premium.

settings.title: UV-sanitised every cycle
settings.text:
Built-in UV light kills bacteria, mould, and odour-causing microbes while clothes dry fresher and cleaner.
Available on the Stratos & Aero models.

settings.heading: Why Nuracom beats the clothesline and the dryer

settings.title: Raise, lower, and light at the tap of a button
settings.body: One remote controls height, lighting, and drying mode — so laundry fits into your day instead of disrupting it.

settings.title: Bright, focused lighting exactly where you need it
settings.body: A laundry area that's actually well-lit. No more squinting at stains or hunting for missing socks in a shadowed corner.

settings.title: Dry faster indoors — without relying on the weather
settings.body: Gentle warm airflow accelerates drying without the fabric damage of a traditional dryer, and without the power bill.
settings.bullet_2: Lower running cost than a dryer

settings.title: Out of sight, out of the way — until you need it
settings.body: Mounted to the ceiling and lowered on demand. Your laundry stays clean, open, and usable even mid-wash.

settings.title: Flexible capacity for the way your family actually does laundry
settings.body: Extend rails for big loads or long garments like sheets and trousers — then retract for compact storage.

settings.heading: A premium laundry upgrade backed by real support
settings.sub: See how Australian families are using Nuracom — and why they keep telling their friends about it.
settings.founder_quote: We built Nuracom for Aussie homes that don't have the room — or the weather — for a traditional clothesline. Every unit ships from our Australian warehouse, and we stand behind it personally. If something ever isn't right, you'll hear back from a real person.

settings.question: Which Nuracom model is right for me?
settings.answer:
Breeze suits small households, studios, and single-person laundries.

Aero is our most popular choice — ideal for families of three to four, and includes premium lighting plus heated drying.

Stratos is built for larger households, premium renovations, and anyone wanting telescopic rails to handle bulky sheets, trousers, and big loads.

settings.question: Does Nuracom replace a dryer?
settings.answer:
For most Australian households, yes. Aero and Stratos include heated drying, and combined with indoor airflow they handle everyday laundry without the power bill of a traditional dryer. Many customers keep a dryer for rare emergencies but stop using it for daily washing.

If you rely on a dryer several times a day through winter, we'd recommend Stratos specifically for its faster heated airflow.

settings.question: Do I need an electrician to install it?
settings.answer:
If you already have the ceiling power point available than No, however if you do not or you don't have the means to easily access one, Yes. Nuracom is ceiling-mounted and hard-wired, so installation must be done by a licensed electrician. Typical install takes 30–60 minutes and costs roughly $150–$300 depending on your area.

In most cases, your sparky will replace an existing ceiling light fitting with the Nuracom unit — so you don't lose lighting, you upgrade it.

settings.question: How much ceiling clearance do I need?
settings.answer: Nuracom sits flush against the ceiling when retracted, so it works with any standard ceiling height of 2.4m or above. At full lower, the unit drops down approximately 1.2–1.4m depending on model, so standing under it is comfortable for most users.

settings.question: Can I install it into a rental or apartment?
settings.answer: Installation involves fixing to the ceiling and wiring into mains, so written landlord or strata consent is usually required. Many renters install it with landlord approval because it replaces a light fitting — we recommend confirming before you order.

settings.question: How long does shipping take?
settings.answer: We ship from our Australian warehouse within one business day of ordering. Metro deliveries typically arrive in 2–4 business days, and regional deliveries in 4–7 business days. You can check your postcode in the Shipping section above.

settings.question: What's the warranty and return policy?
settings.answer:
Every Nuracom comes with:

5 years – motor
2 years – all parts
1 year – full unit service

If anything isn't right, you'll deal directly with a real Australian-based team — not an offshore contact form.

We also offer 30-day returns. If it's not right for your home, send it back for a refund.

settings.question: Is it noisy?
settings.answer: The motor is intentionally quiet — noticeably quieter than a traditional tumble dryer. Our ultra-silent motors are often hard to even hear. Heated drying is designed to operate overnight without disturbing sleep in adjacent rooms.

settings.question: What can I dry on it?
settings.answer:
Everyday clothing, sheets, towels, uniforms, activewear, baby clothes — anything you'd normally hang on a line. Telescopic rails (Stratos) extend to handle long items like king-size sheets without folding.

Each unit has a maximum rated capacity — see the model comparison above for exact limits.

settings.question: How much does it cost to run?
settings.answer:
Less than a dollar per full drying cycle — on every model. At Australia's current average residential electricity rate of $0.3557/kWh, a full 2-hour cycle costs:

  • Breeze — ~$0.57 (800W motor)
  • Aero — ~$0.57 (800W motor)
  • Stratos — ~$0.64 (900W motor)

For comparison, the same load through a standard electric tumble dryer typically costs $1.70–$2.10 — around 3× more. Over a year of regular washing, that difference adds up fast.

These are real numbers based on rated wattage, not marketing estimates.
